text: Remember the first time you used a computer? You could see everything happening on screen.
Now imagine being handed a computer with no display, just inputs and outputs.
That's essentially how we're using AI today. Powerful, but blind.
At Tilda, we've built the controls for AI. We're not just opening the black box.
We're installing Windows and giving you the command you've always wanted.
Now you can see how your AI thinks, why it makes decisions, and most importantly, how to make it better.
Let's dive into a problem-solving task with Lama 3.
Here we're trying to solve a brain teaser that emits a slick math trick.
Unfortunately, the model fails to spot the trick and doesn't give the correct answer.
Then we'll apply Tilda's interventions and show you the difference.
This time, the trick is found and the model nails the brain teaser.
Now let's give a fun example. Video generation.
Again, you'll see both the plain model-generated video and also the Tilda-steered outputs.
Notice the improvement in accuracy and adherence to user preferences.
At Tilda, we see interpretability as the gateway to unlocking new levels of AI performance without compromising on safety.
This is just the tip of the iceberg and we're excited to share more of what we're working on.
If this sounds interesting to you, come join us.
